metadata.greeting: This weekend is packed with exciting activities for your little one in and around Hackney! Get ready for a fun-filled adventure with interactive theatre, vibrant festivals, and engaging play areas. There's something for everyone to enjoy!

activities.title: Haringey Shed's 25th Anniversary – The Silver Hive
activities.description: Celebrate Haringey Shed's 25th anniversary with 'The Silver Hive' at Alexandra Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun, celebratory event suitable for all ages, offering a chance to engage with local arts and culture.

transport.fromHackney: Take the W3 bus from Hackney Central to Alexandra Palace Station
transport.tip: Check the event schedule in advance for timings.

activities.title: The Together Space March Family Event
activities.description: A World Book Day-themed event with cooking workshops, collage table, weightlifting, book swaps, sensory space, active play area, soft play, and inflatable activity run.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides diverse activities suitable for a 3-year-old, including sensory play, active movement, and creative workshops.

transport.fromHackney: Check specific location for best route, but likely a local bus or Overground
transport.tip: Book in advance as these events are popular.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Indoor/outdoor play space to inspire imagination and storytelling with a musical forest, space ship, and story garden.
activities.whyItWorks: Designed for young children with spaces to inspire imagination and storytelling. Ideal for ages 3-5.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: Museum of childhood with collections of toys, costumes and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging exhibits perfect for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Bethnal Green.
transport.tip: Check for special events or workshops.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: Small, beautifully designed wooden playground with a pond theme.
activities.whyItWorks: Perfect for toddlers with its engaging design and safe environment.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Stratford.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the nearby Olympic Park.

activities.title: Mome London - Creative Play & Café
activities.description: Bright, creative indoor play space designed for babies and toddlers with soft play zones, sensory areas, and a café.
activities.whyItWorks: Specifically designed for toddlers with calm, curated play areas and a parent-friendly café.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus to Angel, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book ahead for weekend sessions.

activities.title: Half Moon Theatre - Cinema Chill: Peace at Last
activities.description: Watch a digital opera performance on the big screen in a relaxed environment.  Mr. Bear tries to sleep amidst various sounds.
activities.whyItWorks: Relaxed environment suitable for young children, opera is visual and engaging.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus towards Mile End, then walk.
transport.tip: Check for any age-specific relaxed performances.

activities.title: Battersea Power Station Mr Men Little Miss Takeover
activities.description: Interactive activities including a treasure hunt to discover familiar characters from Mr. Men/Little Miss.
activities.whyItWorks: Interactive and engaging activities for young children. Free admission.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Battersea Power Station (Northern Line).
transport.tip: Check specific event timings on the website.

activities.title: Zoonormous Egg Hunt at London Zoo
activities.description: Easter egg hunt searching for giant eggs hidden around the zoo. Also, Easter craft activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines animal viewing with an engaging Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Euston, then a bus to London Zoo.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: The Lindt Gold Bunny Hunt at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Walk through the gardens, looking for Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues. Chocolate prize at the end.
activities.whyItWorks: Motivates kids to walk with an Easter-themed activity and a chocolate reward.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Go early in the day for a calmer experience.

activities.title: The Magic Garden at Hampton Court Palace
activities.description: Cool playground that provides a unique way for the family to explore the history of the Palace.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun playground set within a historical context.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and train to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Allow plenty of time to explore both the playground and the palace.

activities.title: Kew Gardens – Octonauts Above and Beyond
activities.description: An interactive outdoor mission to complete challenges and learn how to protect our planet with the Octonauts.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines outdoor exploration with a popular children's theme.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and tube to Kew Gardens.
transport.tip: Allow 2-3 hours for a proper visit.

activities.title: Cutty Sark Easter Egg Trail
activities.description: Easter Egg trail where children follow the trail to spot hidden eggs around the ship and unscramble a secret nautical code for a chocolate treat.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines exploring a historic ship with an Easter activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Cutty Sark station.
transport.tip: Suitable for young explorers age five+ and their families.

activities.title: Postal Museum & Mail Rail
activities.price: £17 adults, £10 children (3-15), under 3s free. Separate ticket for Postal Play Space
activities.description: Interactive exhibits, including a play space and Mail Rail ride.
activities.whyItWorks: Hands-on exhibits, a dedicated play space for younger children, and a unique underground train ride.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus and tube to Farringdon, then a short walk.
transport.tip: Book a 45-minute play session for Sorted! in advance.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.address: Exhibition Road, South Kensington, London, SW7 2DD
activities.price: Free, some exhibitions may require tickets
activities.description: Explore interactive exhibits related to science and technology.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging and educational for all ages, with hands-on exhibits.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to South Kensington.
transport.tip: Check out Wonderlab for a dedicated interactive experience for kids.

activities.title: Fairy Tales at the British Library
activities.description: Interactive exhibition exploring enchanted lands, magical creatures, and timeless tales through books, costumes, and puppets.
activities.whyItWorks: Thoughtfully designed and immersive without being overwhelming. Interactive elements suitable for young children.

transport.fromHackney: Take a bus or Overground to Euston.
transport.tip: Book early time slots at weekends.

activities.title: Tower Bridge Easter Family Activities
activities.description: Discover phenakistoscopes, the original animations and create your own of Tower Bridge.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative, historical, and hands on activity.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Tower Hill.
transport.tip: These activities are scheduled on specific dates, so plan accordingly.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun at Southbank Centre
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families with shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break.
activities.whyItWorks: Variety of activities to suit different interests and ages, including free options.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Study the program beforehand and make bookings so you don't miss out.

activities.title: REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground
activities.description: A play space made out of repurposed materials.
activities.whyItWorks: Creative and sustainable play space.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and tube to Waterloo.
transport.tip: Check for relaxed sessions for a more sensory-friendly experience.

activities.title: Paddington Bear Experience
activities.price: Adult £34, children £24. Easter experiences are £15 extra
activities.description: An adventure through London and Peru with interactive sets, live performances, and themed activities inspired by Paddington Bear. Easter Egg hunt through Windsor Gardens and an Easter basket of treats for Easter.
activities.whyItWorks: Immersive and interactive experience based on a beloved character.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and tube to a Southbank station (e.g., Waterloo).
transport.tip: Easter experiences cost extra, so factor that into your budget.

activities.title: Disney on Ice – Into the Magic
activities.dates: 25th March – 12th March (Conflicting dates in source, check dates)
activities.description: Be inspired by Disney's Magical characters.
activities.whyItWorks: Visual spectacle and familiar characters.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground and tube to Wembley Park.
transport.tip: Conflicting dates, double check before booking.

activities.title: London Transport Museum
activities.description: Exhibits about the history of London's transport system.
activities.whyItWorks: Engaging exhibits about transport.

transport.fromHackney: Take the tube to Covent Garden.
transport.tip: Check for family activities.

activities.title: Who Let the Gods Out
activities.description: A theatre production suitable for young adventurers.
activities.whyItWorks: Fun for older children.

transport.fromHackney: Tube and train to Wimbledon.
transport.tip: Best for older children
